name: IAM Roles and Service Accounts
description: Create service accounts, assign IAM roles, and manage permissions for secure access to GCP resources.
trendingReason: Least-privilege IAM is critical as organizations tighten cloud security postures.
prerequisites: Project Owner or IAM Admin role

steps.title: Create a service account
steps.command: gcloud iam service-accounts create myapp-sa --display-name "My App Service Account" --description "Service account for myapp"
steps.description: Creates a service account that applications use to authenticate to GCP APIs.

steps.title: Grant a role to the service account
steps.description: Assigns the Storage Object Viewer role, granting read-only access to Cloud Storage objects.
steps.tip: Follow the principle of least privilege. Grant the narrowest role that meets the requirement.

steps.title: Create and download a key file
steps.command: gcloud iam service-accounts keys create key.json --iam-account myapp-sa@my-project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com
steps.description: Generates a JSON key file for authenticating as the service account.
steps.warning: Key files are long-lived credentials. Prefer Workload Identity Federation for production. Never commit key files to version control.

steps.title: List IAM policy bindings
steps.description: Displays all role bindings in the project in a readable table.

steps.title: Revoke a role
steps.description: Removes the specified role from the service account.

fullScript:
# IAM roles and service accounts
gcloud iam service-accounts create myapp-sa --display-name "My App Service Account" --description "Service account for myapp"
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding my-project-id --member "serviceAccount:myapp-sa@my-project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com" --role "roles/storage.objectViewer"
gcloud iam service-accounts keys create key.json --iam-account myapp-sa@my-project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com
gcloud projects get-iam-policy my-project-id --flatten="bindings[].members" --format="table(bindings.role, bindings.members)"
gcloud projects remove-iam-policy-binding my-project-id --member "serviceAccount:myapp-sa@my-project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com" --role "roles/storage.objectViewer"

faq.question: What is Workload Identity Federation?
faq.answer: Workload Identity Federation lets external workloads (e.g., GitHub Actions, AWS) authenticate to GCP without service account keys by exchanging external tokens for short-lived GCP credentials.

faq.question: How do I find which roles a service account has?
faq.answer: Use 'gcloud asset search-all-iam-policies --query="policy:myapp-sa@my-project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com"' to find all IAM bindings across the project.

faq.question: What is the difference between basic and predefined roles?
faq.answer: Basic roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer) are broad and grant wide access. Predefined roles like 'roles/storage.objectViewer' are fine-grained and follow least-privilege principles.
